flashcards.front: What are the three main categories of Java control statements?
flashcards.back: 1. Selection (if, switch) 2. Iteration (for, while, do-while) 3. Jump (break, continue, return)

flashcards.front: How does JSP implement conditional logic without scriptlets?
flashcards.back: Using JSTL tags like <c:if> and <c:choose> from the Core JSTL library.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the break statement in a switch construct?
flashcards.back: Prevents fall-through to subsequent cases. Without break, execution continues to next case.

flashcards.front: How to read request parameters in JSP control statements?
flashcards.back: Use request.getParameter("name") with the implicit request object.

flashcards.front: What's the difference between while and do-while loops?
flashcards.back: do-while executes body at least once before checking condition, while checks condition first.

flashcards.front: How to implement a for loop in JSP using scriptlets?
flashcards.back: <% for(int i=0; i<5; i++) { %> HTML content <% } %>

flashcards.front: What JSP directive is required for using JSTL tags?
flashcards.back: <%@ taglib uri="http://java.sun.com/jsp/jstl/core" prefix="c" %>

flashcards.front: Purpose of enhanced for loop in Java?
flashcards.back: Iterate through arrays/collections without index management. Syntax: for(Type item : collection)

flashcards.front: How to handle multiple conditions in JSP?
flashcards.back: Use JSTL <c:choose> with <c:when> and <c:otherwise> tags.

flashcards.front: What happens if no default case in switch statement?
flashcards.back: Program continues execution after switch block if no matching cases found.

flashcards.front: How to set cookies in Servlet for session control?
flashcards.back: Cookie cookie = new Cookie(name, value); response.addCookie(cookie);

flashcards.front: Why avoid scriptlets for control flow in JSP?
flashcards.back: Mixes Java/HTML, reduces maintainability. Use JSTL/EL for separation of concerns.

flashcards.front: Syntax for if-else in JSP using scriptlets
flashcards.back: <% if(condition) { %>Content<% } else { %>Alt Content<% } %>

flashcards.front: What is short-circuit evaluation in Java?
flashcards.back: In && and || operators: stops evaluation once outcome is determined (left to right).

flashcards.front: How to iterate over cookies in Servlet?
flashcards.back: Cookie[] cookies = request.getCookies(); for(Cookie c : cookies) {...}

flashcards.front: Purpose of <jsp:useBean> in control flow?
flashcards.back: Instantiates JavaBean for data processing in JSP control statements.

flashcards.front: How to exit nested loops in Java?
flashcards.back: Use labeled break: outer: while(...) { break outer; }

flashcards.front: What JSTL tag replaces scriptlet for loops?
flashcards.back: <c:forEach items="${collection}" var="item">Content</c:forEach>

flashcards.front: How to read init parameters in Servlet?
flashcards.back: String value = getServletConfig().getInitParameter("param");

flashcards.front: Purpose of continue statement in loops?
flashcards.back: Skips current iteration and proceeds to next iteration of the loop.

flashcards.front: How to implement switch-like logic in JSTL?
flashcards.back: Use <c:choose> with multiple <c:when> tags and one <c:otherwise>.

flashcards.front: What is the ternary operator syntax in Java?
flashcards.back: variable = (condition) ? valueIfTrue : valueIfFalse;

flashcards.front: How to handle null in JSP control statements?
flashcards.back: Use EL empty operator: <c:if test="${empty variable}">Content</c:if>

flashcards.front: Difference between include directive and action?
flashcards.back: Directive (<%@ include %>) at compile-time, Action (<jsp:include>) at runtime.

flashcards.front: How to redirect using JSP control flow?
flashcards.back: Use response.sendRedirect("url") in scriptlet or <c:redirect url="..."/> in JSTL.
